summary: A pristine soundboard capture of the classic June 8 1974 Oakland Coliseum show, highlighted by a monster Playing‑in‑the‑Band/Wharf‑Rat sandwich and fiery jams.
review: The Miller‑remastered soundboard delivers crisp, immersive audio that puts listeners back in the stadium, with only occasional tuning quirks. Musically the performance is tight and inspired – the legendary Playing > Wharf Rat > Playing sequence, soaring China Cat Sunflower, blazing Eyes of the World and punchy Scarlet Begonias make this a standout 1974 concert.

ai_rating.rationale: All ten reviews are overwhelmingly positive (9‑star, 1 four‑star) and praise both the musical performance and the high‑quality soundboard remaster; minor tuning/vocal issues do not detract from the overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered by Charlie Miller, crisp and balanced with good audience immersion; a few reviewers note occasional guitar tuning slip or harsh Donna Jean wails but no major drop‑outs or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Classic 1974 set with seamless segues, especially the unique Playing‑in‑the‑Band → Wharf Rat → Playing sandwich; pacing stays tight and energetic throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for wild, experimental playing – feedback sections, slide work on "Wolf" and overall high energy.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as tight, inspirational, with standout bass leads in the PITB sandwich and strong vocal contributions.
band_member_comments.Bob: His on‑stage banter is noted as relaxed and non‑chalant, adding to the laid‑back vibe.
